title: Event Envelope v1
description: One append-only JSONL record. The event log is the audit + replay source of truth: projections (L3) and indexes (L4) are rebuilt from L0-L2 + events. APPEND-ONLY — events are NEVER rewritten or deleted; corrections are new events (e.g. fact_superseded). Forward-compatible: unknown fields are preserved on replay. New v1 kinds are additive and bump the schema minor version.

ulid.description: Canonical ULID: 26-char Crockford base32, time-sortable, immutable.

stream.description: Which append-only JSONL stream this record belongs to (capture_events / memory_events / projection_events).

kind.description: Event kind. Allowed values are constrained per stream (see allOf).

occurred_at.description: When the event logically happened (ISO-8601, UTC recommended).

recorded_at.description: When the event was appended to the log. May differ from occurred_at for imports/backfills.

actor.description: Who caused the event: 'human', 'cli', 'skill:<name>', or 'sidecar:<name>'. Never silent/anonymous.

subject_id.description: Id of the note/fact/entity this event concerns. Required for capture and memory events; optional for vault-wide projection events.

source_ref.description: Provenance pointer (e.g. the raw note id a fact was extracted from) when applicable.

schema_version.description: Optional semver of the event envelope this record was written against (e.g. '1.0.0').

payload.description: Kind-specific data. Domain-neutral. Free-form; readers must tolerate unknown keys.

allOf.$comment: capture stream — raw material entered the system.
projection stream — rebuildable L3/L4 derived state changed; may be vault-wide.
